title: Working for UKHO
body: "The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) offers flexible working and challenging roles. From hydrography and data science to software and product development, we work together to deliver quality, innovative maritime navigation solutions as a world-leading centre for hydrography.  Based in the heart of Taunton, Somerset, our purpose-built office provides an open and dynamic workplace for our people. We encourage a culture focused on performance over presence, offering hybrid working to promote work-life balance and well-being.  Each team at the UKHO has unique skills and responsibilities that contribute to our purpose: supporting safe, secure and thriving oceans.  Vacancies   View vacancies at the UKHO.   Apprenticeships  Our award-winning apprenticeship scheme offers practical experience as a paid employee while furthering your education and gaining an industry-recognised qualification.  Learn about our apprenticeships.  Apply for available apprenticeships.  Year in Industry Placements  We offer year-long industry placements for undergraduates studying degree courses between their second and third years.  You will join our Operations teams, working alongside and learning from subject matter experts to deliver services or products for customers whose safety at sea relies on the accuracy and timeliness of the information the UK Hydrographic Office provides.  Placements start at the end of June. We advertise placements from early December, with selection in late January and early February.  View vacancies at the UKHO.  Benefits  We pride ourselves on offering an excellent working environment with forward-thinking policies and reward structures that benefit our community.   Generous holidays with 26.5 days annual leave, rising to 31.5 days after five years Maternity, adoption and shared parental leave and an on-site nursery Flexible working arrangements Outstanding pension contribution rates up to 27.9% Employee discount schemes, vouchers and recognition awards Award-winning office with an on-site gym and canteen   Learn about life at the UKHO. "
